elements.name: Title
texts: "The fame of that place has been so scattered through European parts that it seems to go on wings"

elements.name: Description
texts: "It is more certain than certainty that in the northern shore of Ireland a place horrible by its terrors was found by him and is generally called St Patrick's Purgatory. The fame of that place has been so scattered through European parts that it seems to go on wings."

elements.name: Source
texts: Thyraeus: Panegyrics on the Names, Tribulations and Miracles of St Patrick. Douai, 1616. Leslie, Shane, Saint Patrick's Purgatory: A Record from History and Literature, p. v

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourne Ltd, London

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "It is more certain than certainty that in the northern shore of Ireland a place horrible by its terrors was found by him and is generally called St Patrick's Purgatory. The fame of that place has been so scattered through European parts that it seems to go on wings."
